mission.title: Install and Configure Vultr Kubernetes Engine (VKE)
mission.description: This mission guides you through installing the Vultr Kubernetes Engine (VKE), a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies cluster management on Vultr's infrastructure. It automates resource provisioning and integrates Vultr's features seamlessly into your Kubernetes environment. Key trade-offs include reliance on Vultr's infrastructure and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the Vultr CLI
steps.description:
The Vultr CLI is required to interact with the Vultr API for creating and managing your Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/vultr/vultr-cli/releases/download/v1.0.0/vultr-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf vultr-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv vultr /usr/local/bin/

vultr version

```

> Download the latest Vultr CLI
> Extract the downloaded tarball
> Move the binary to your PATH
> Verify the installation

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Vultr with the specified version and node count.

```bash

vultr k8s create --region 'ewr' --version '1.30' --node-count 3 --plan 'vc2-4c-8gb'

```

> Replace 'ewr' with your preferred region

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

vultr k8s kubeconfig --cluster-id <your-cluster-id> > ~/.kube/config

```

> Replace <your-cluster-id> with your actual cluster ID

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
Checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up monitoring and autoscaling for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloudprovider/vultr/examples/cluster-autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/metrics-server/master/deploy/1.8+/metrics-server-deployment.yaml

```

> Deploy the Cluster Autoscaler
> Deploy the Metrics Server for monitoring

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes cluster
uninstall.description:
This will remove the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

vultr k8s delete --cluster-id <your-cluster-id>

```

> Replace <your-cluster-id> with your actual cluster ID

uninstall.title: Clean up orphaned resources
uninstall.description:
This step ensures that any orphaned resources like load balancers and persistent volumes are removed.

```bash

vultr load-balancer delete --all

vultr volume delete --all

```

> Delete all load balancers
> Delete all volumes

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This step upgrades the cluster to the latest version.

```bash

vultr k8s upgrade --cluster-id <your-cluster-id> --version '1.31'

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

```

> Replace <your-cluster-id> with your actual cluster ID
> Drain the node before upgrading
> Uncordon the node after the upgrade

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Verify your kubeconfig file and ensure the cluster is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources allocated to schedule new pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or upgrade the node plan.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'LoadBalancer service is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the load balancer is not provisioned correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the service configuration and ensure the cloud controller manager is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that a pod is failing to start due to application errors.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to diagnose the issue.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes version 1.30, improved load balancer provisioning.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' status, indicating that your cluster is operational.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Vultr CLI and kubectl installed, and your environment meets the resource requirements.
